purpose: Determine whether Gate A (PGlite) and Gate B (Supabase-compatible role model) from the paused ACTIVATION-2 attempt can be reused as-is, per section 4's explicit reuse conditions, rather than replayed.

gate_a_pglite.utc_bucket_test_detail: generated_column test confirms bucket = 2026-07-16T10:00:00.000Z; timezone_stability test confirms the value is unchanged after SET TIME ZONE 'America/New_York'
gate_a_pglite.append_only_db_level: LIMITED -- PGlite has a single superuser role, cannot test anon/authenticated/service_role separation; explicitly deferred to Gate B by Gate A's own report
gate_a_pglite.rls_role_based_enforcement: not tested (PGlite has no Supabase role model) -- explicitly deferred to Gate B
gate_a_pglite.reuse_basis: covers apply/rerun/rollback/clean-reapply/UTC-bucket-immutability in full; hashes verified identical to current files

gate_b_supabase_compatible.rls_test_detail: anon/authenticated SELECT return 0 rows with no error; anon/authenticated INSERT explicitly rejected with a row-security violation error; service_role (BYPASSRLS) succeeds on both
gate_b_supabase_compatible.apply_rerun_rollback_clean_reapply_docum: apply only (single pass) -- rerun/rollback/clean-reapply not repeated here by design, already fully covered by Gate A
gate_b_supabase_compatible.utc_bucket_test_note: not this gate's purpose -- already covered by Gate A
gate_b_supabase_compatible.append_only_db_level: PARTIALLY_ADDRESSED -- confirms anon/authenticated cannot write at all (RLS-level), but does not additionally REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE from service_role, per the documented, pre-existing design choice in docs/MARKET_INDEX_SECURITY_AND_RLS.md (append-only enforced at the TypeScript repository-interface level, not by a DB-level REVOKE on service_role)
gate_b_supabase_compatible.hash_verification_gap_found: Gate B's own JSON records the 5 migration filenames it applied but does not persist their SHA-256 inline.
gate_b_supabase_compatible.hash_verification_gap_resolution: Not extrapolated. Resolved via git chain-of-custody instead of assumption: `git log --follow` on all 5 files shows the last commit touching any of them is e9f4d7a (the UTC fix), and Gate B's own generated_at_utc (2026-07-16T22:55:00Z) is after e9f4d7a and before every subsequent commit (7b068b8, 6b340ef, 442b9c0), none of which touch supabase/migrations/2026071614*.sql (confirmed via git diff HEAD returning zero changes on all 5 files at every step of the quarantine mission). Gate A, run moments before Gate B in the same session from the same unmodified directory, explicitly recorded these same 5 hashes, and they are confirmed identical to the current file hashes above. Therefore Gate B necessarily applied byte-identical content to what exists now, proven by git history rather than assumed.
gate_b_supabase_compatible.reuse_basis: the one required proof that was not inline (hash) is supplied by git provenance across the full commit chain, not by extrapolation; RLS role-based enforcement -- Gate B's actual purpose -- is fully documented

joint_coverage_assessment.conclusion: Gate A and Gate B are complementary by design, not independent duplicates. Together they cover every item in section 4's checklist: apply/rerun/rollback/clean-reapply (Gate A), UTC bucket immutability (Gate A), PostgreSQL version disclosed (both), and anon/authenticated/service_role RLS enforcement (Gate B, the gap Gate A's own report explicitly flagged as LIMITED and handed off). No item in the checklist is unaddressed by the pair.
joint_coverage_assessment.decision: REUSE both gates. No replay required.

no_market_index_migration_file_changed_s.method: git log --oneline --follow on each of the 5 files, from Gate A/B's generation time to now
no_market_index_migration_file_changed_s.result: 0 commits touch any of the 5 files after e9f4d7a (which predates both gates)
